purpose: Reconcile Grave base normal-fire AR damage in clean isolation (solo, Burst II, ZERO Full Bursts possible). Cleanest rider-free AR-normal probe.

fight_window.notes: Single continuous fight, no aborted attempts. Combat clock starts ~7s into video (clock read 02:07 at video t=60).

total_damage.other_slots: all NO SIGNAL / EMPTY SLOT, 0 damage

burst.evidence: Green Burst-I hex indicator visible on right edge throughout entire fight, never advances; lone Burst-II unit cannot complete the I->II->III chain. Entire output is normal AR fire.

boss_element.interpretation: No elemental-advantage up-arrow marks on any damage number; treated as neutral matchup for Grave (Fire).

fire_cadence.evidence: Ammo counter (displayed at crosshair) counts down exactly 3 rounds per 0.25s (057->054->051->048...->012->009->006->003->000) with no acceleration across multiple magazines.
fire_cadence.notes: Fire is frequently interrupted mid-magazine by target-reacquisition pauses (boss walking); ammo holds during the pause, no rounds lost.

multiplier_ladder.note: Ladder factors exactly off base 20787: 20787x1.5=31180 (crit), x2.0=41573 (core), x2.5=51966 (core+crit). Core = exactly 2x normal, matching the given core-hit fact. Base itself varies with boss range (~17998 far to ~20787 near).

counter_reconciliation.method: damage-counter delta over a verified full 60-round magazine (ammo 060->000), divided by 60

magazines.notes: Fired 48 + reacquire pause + 12 = 60 rounds. Low-core segment.

core_fraction.method: backed out from avg_per_shot vs base 20787 assuming additive core(+1.0)/crit(+0.5) multipliers and ~15% crit rate
core_fraction.notes: Core fraction is highly variable and aim/range-dependent. Confounded by range-dependent base (17998-20787). Direct popup sampling shows a substantial but non-dominant mix of red CORE HIT vs white normal popups.
core_fraction.confidence: medium (variability is real; absolute level uncertain due to range-varying base)

fight_wide.method: total / 3-mag-mean per-shot; est_total_shots derived, not directly counted

reload_remeasure.purpose: Directly resolve the shot-count question: sim fires ~1620 shots; a per-shot damage estimate suggested ~1250. Measure the TRUE total shots, reload count, and real reload-to-resume downtime.

method.primary_signal: Frame-differenced the FIXED top-center TOTAL DAMAGE counter box (crop 380x40 @ 1110,46) at 60fps over the whole 194.7s video. Each landed shot commits one instantaneous counter update = one isolated 1-frame diff spike; static (~0 diff) between shots. Peak-count = literal per-shot event count (NOT a magnitude back-out).
method.validation: Spike detection cross-checked against the two independently hand-read magazines in this file: mag2 window (t103.4-110.3) = exactly 60 spikes; a late window (t168-176) = exactly 60 spikes at clean 5-frame spacing. No missed shots even in low-damage/far-range segments (relative digit change stays visible). Muzzle-flash brightness (secondary) confirmed firing ceases during the measured reload gaps.
method.shot_cadence_confirmed: Spikes land every 5 frames = 12.0 rounds/s, steady (matches existing fire_cadence).

total_shots.notes: Raw 60fps spike count 1247 includes ~15-20 non-shot artifacts (pre-fight UI settling t1.4-3.8, pause-menu open/close transitions at t~119, post-fight result-screen at t>190). After removing those and correcting +-1/2 per-mag miscounts: ~1230. Structural cross-check: 20 emptied magazines x60 + ~25-shot final partial = ~1225.
total_shots.verdict: DECISIVELY confirms the ~1250 camp, NOT the sim's ~1620. Real shots ~1230, roughly 76% of sim.

reload_count.notes: 20 unambiguous reload gaps separating 21 magazine bursts (20 full 60-round mags each followed by a reload + 1 final partial mag at fight end, never reloaded). Fight starts on a full 60 mag; each mag confirmed to empty to ~000 (reload progress-bar visible at reticle at t=64.75).

reload_to_resume_gap.headline: REAL reload-to-resume downtime = 3.35s (201 frames), median across 19 clean events. This is ~2.5x the 81-frame (1.35s) datasheet reload spec.
reload_to_resume_gap.measured_as: Frames from the LAST shot's damage-commit (=last bullet, hitscan AR lands instantly) to the FIRST shot's damage-commit after the reload. Captures the FULL downtime: reload animation + re-aim/spin-up before fire resumes.

other_firing_pauses.notes: Outside reloads, ~10-13s of firing lost to mid-magazine target-reacquisition pauses (boss walking; ammo HOLDS, no rounds lost) occurring in ~8 magazines at ~1.3-2.0s each. Separately, the player opened the Shooting-Range pause menu once at t~119-120.9 (~1.7s). Derived as sum over each mag of (segment span - shots/12).

time_budget_reconciliation.note: Firing(1230/12=102.5) + reloads(20x3.35=67) + reacquire(~11) = ~180.5s, closing to the 180s fight clock. Independent confirmation of all three numbers.
time_budget_reconciliation.root_cause_of_sim_overfire: Sim assumes 1.35s reloads (~6.35s cycle -> ~28 mags -> ~1620 shots). Real cycle is ~8.27s (5.0s dump + 3.35s reload gap) -> ~21 mags -> ~1230 shots. The 2.0s-per-reload extra downtime (3.35 real vs 1.35 spec), x20 reloads = ~40s = ~480 fewer shots, is the dominant cause.

recording_notes.video_contains_one_player_pause: t~119-120.9 (Shooting-Range pause menu); does not affect the fight clock alignment materially.
